name: Surrounding the Rio Guadahortuna — circular via Alamedilla and Dehesas de Guadix
name_original: Rodeando el Río Guadahortuna — circular por Alamedilla y Dehesas de Guadix
region_intro: Road cycling around Guadahortuna offers diverse terrain within the Granada province of Andalusia, characterized by varied landscapes suitable for different cycling preferences. The region is part of the Granada Geopark, featuring distinctive rock formations and agricultural paths. Cyclists can explore routes along the Río Guadahortuna, through vast farmlands, and near the rugged Sierra Mágina Natural Park. This area provides both challenging mountain ascents and tranquil inland roads. Best road cycling routes around Guadahortuna The most popular road cycling route is Surrounding the Rio Guadahortuna — circular via Alamedilla and Dehesas de Guadix, a 49.6 miles (79.9 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 38 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the fertile river valley and surrounding agricultural areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra Mágina Park Loop, a difficult 61.3 miles (98.7 km) path. This route offers significant elevation gain through the rugged landscapes bordering the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. Local road cyclists also love the Roman Dam of Barcinas – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Montejícar, a 110.6 miles (178.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain including reservoirs and historical points, often completed in about 8 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Guadahortuna is defined by river valleys, extensive farmlands, and the geological features of the Granada Geopark. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Guadahortuna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 270 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Guadahortuna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Guadahortuna?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Guadahortuna, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes traverse varied landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to tranquil inland roads through farmlands and orchards, catering to different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Guadahortuna?
A: Road cycling around Guadahortuna offers a mix of terrain. You'll find routes through the fertile river valleys and extensive farmlands, as well as more challenging climbs in the rugged landscapes bordering the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. The region is also part of the Granada Geopark, meaning you might encounter unique geological features and agricultural paths.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While many routes in the area are considered difficult, there are 5 moderate road cycling routes available around Guadahortuna. These routes offer a less strenuous experience while still allowing you to enjoy the region's scenic beauty.

Q: What are some of the most scenic road cycling routes in the area?
A: For a scenic ride exploring the fertile river valley and agricultural areas, consider the circular route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986735530/surrounding-the-rio-guadahortuna-circular-via-alamedilla-and-dehesas-de-guadix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Surrounding the Rio Guadahortuna — circular via Alamedilla and Dehesas de Guadix&lt;/a&gt;. Another option for breathtaking views is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986804089/sierra-magina-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Mágina Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant elevation gain through rugged landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by interesting landmarks. You might encounter historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5310415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Hacho&lt;/a&gt;, an old railway bridge over the Río Guadahortuna. Other points of interest in the wider area include the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5035520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznalloz&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/896059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Cabra&lt;/a&gt; waterfall.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Guadahortuna?
A: The Granada region, including Guadahortuna, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in inland areas, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are generally mild, though higher elevations might experience cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986735530/surrounding-the-rio-guadahortuna-circular-via-alamedilla-and-dehesas-de-guadix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Surrounding the Rio Guadahortuna — circular via Alamedilla and Dehesas de Guadix&lt;/a&gt;. Another longer circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38210162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Barcinas – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Montejícar&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied terrain.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Guadahortuna?
A: The road cycling routes around Guadahortuna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from tranquil farmlands to challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore the unique geological features of the Granada Geopark.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging routes for experienced road cyclists?
A: Experienced road cyclists will find several challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38210162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Barcinas – Cubillas Reservoir loop from Montejícar&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 110.6-mile route with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14664357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Barcinas – View of Chulilla loop from Píñar&lt;/a&gt;, offering a substantial ride through varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the road cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes or pubs directly on every route may vary, Guadahortuna and the surrounding towns offer local establishments where cyclists can refuel. The region's agricultural character means you'll often pass through villages with amenities. Planning your route with potential stops in mind is always a good idea.

Q: Is it possible to find accommodation suitable for cyclists in Guadahortuna?
A: Guadahortuna and nearby towns provide various accommodation options, from rural guesthouses to small hotels, which can cater to cyclists. It's advisable to check for places that offer secure bike storage or are generally cyclist-friendly when booking.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Guadahortuna?
A: While many routes in the immediate vicinity of Guadahortuna are rated moderate to difficult, the broader region's vast farmlands and less-trafficked inland roads can offer more relaxed sections. For family-friendly options, look for routes that stick to flatter agricultural paths or designated greenways, which may require exploring slightly outside the most challenging mountain areas.
tips: Road that climbs from the center of Alicún de Ortega through what is called the Sierra de Larra, a set of mountains with little vegetation and gray terrain that offer a different and arid landscape. The views from the road to Barranco del Enebro are beautiful.

Alamedilla is a municipality in the Province of Granada and it is crossed by the Río Guadahortuna, a river that originates in the Sierra de Lucena and which passes by is named after the town that is located to the west of the town of Alamedilla.

The Puente del Hacho is a metallic viaduct that crosses the course of the Guadahortuna river. It is located between the towns of Guadahortuna and Alamedilla and is an impressive metal structure built at the end of the 19th century by French engineers.
